title: herorchis picta var. albanica
intro: Herorchis picta var. albanica, also known as the Albanian herorchis or Dorman's orchid, is a rare and endangered species of terrestrial orchid native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Herorchis picta var. albanica include its potential to treat various ailments such as respiratory issues, wounds, and fever, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its availability and use are limited due to its rarity and endangered status.
